seo.title: Young AI Leaders Gweru | Youth AI Hub — Zimbabwe & Africa
seo.h1: Young AI Leaders Gweru Hub — Youth AI, Tech & Social Innovation in Gweru, Zimbabwe & Across Africa
seo.description: Youth-led AI hub in Gweru, Zimbabwe — young leaders building ethical AI, digital innovation and social impact for communities across Africa.
seo.intro: Young AI Leaders Gweru Hub is a Zimbabwe-based node in the global African youth tech movement. We connect young innovators across Africa through ethical artificial intelligence, community technology, social innovation and hands-on AI literacy — rooted in African languages, local context and youth leadership.
seo.ogImageAlt: Young AI Leaders Community logo — Gweru Hub

items.label: Our mission
items.detail: Empowering Gweru’s youth to build ethical, responsible and African-rooted AI that is localised for our languages, our context and Africa’s unique challenges.

items.label: The hub
items.detail: A tight-knit African youth tech community of young AI leaders in Gweru, Midlands Province  collaborating on real projects with community impact across Zimbabwe and Southern Africa.

items.label: AI for Good network
items.detail: We are the Gweru Hub of the global Young AI Leaders Community, an ITU AI for Good initiative connecting African youth tech hubs with innovators worldwide.

items.label: AI Literacy for All
items.detail: Webinar and physical sessions that focused on equiping audience with the knowledge of AI tools and best practices .

body: Looking at people in the sorrounding community , we realised that people only used a handful number of tools. We worked on introducing them to various tools and showcasing their potential

Using a few tools we showcased how people could use different tools for more than chatting. The sessions included sharing tools that asssist with research , study and media generation and techniques to get the best output from them.
We also ended the sessions by highlighting the dangers of AI and how they could be countered and catered for.

items.label: Hub leadership
items.detail: Meet organising team and impact leadership driving Africa-facing tech innovation from Gweru, 2025–2027.

items.label: Our members
items.detail: African youth in tech — students, developers, researchers and change-makers — each committed to at least one community impact project per year.

items.label: Join the hub
items.detail: Applications open through the AI for Good Young AI Leaders programme. Bring your ideas — we’ll bring the community.

leadership.name: Brandon T. Bande
bio: Leads the Young AI Leaders Gweru Hub (2025–2027 cohort), driving Africa-facing AI and tech innovation from Gweru, Midlands Province.

leadership.name: Linda Mapindlo
bio: Part of the hub's organising team, coordinating workshops, events and day-to-day operations for the Gweru Hub.

leadership.name: Webnergy Simango
bio: Part of the hub's organising team, coordinating workshops, events and day-to-day operations for the Gweru Hub.

leadership.name: Mukudzeishe Primrose Tarugarira
bio: Leads impact and community outcomes for the hub, tracking how projects translate into real, measurable change across Gweru. Also doubles as our social media manager.

items.label: Social media
items.detail: Follow the Gweru Hub on LinkedIn, X and Instagram for events, workshops and project updates.

items.label: Find us
items.detail: Gweru, Midlands Province, Zimbabwe  a growing African tech hub at the heart of the country, now a node in the global AI and innovation map.

items.label: Our community
items.detail: An open, inclusive African youth tech space in Gweru — students, creatives, developers and dreamers united by curiosity about AI and a drive for local impact.

items.label: Get involved
items.detail: Volunteer at tech workshops, mentor a beginner, or bring a project idea. Every skill level is welcome — Africa's next generation of innovators grows here.

items.label: Outreach
items.detail: Taking AI and tech literacy beyond the hub: school visits, rural outreach and community demos across the Midlands and wider Zimbabwe.

items.label: Build with AI Buildathon · Southern Africa Jam
items.detail: Thu, Apr 30 · Midlands State University, Gweru — a satellite session of the regional AI buildathon.

more.meta: Thu, Apr 30 · Midlands State University Main Campus, Room 59, Gweru
body: A satellite session of the regional Build with AI Buildathon, run with Data Science MSU — connect, learn and build alongside fellow innovators.
Open to beginners and experienced developers alike: a collaborative hacking session focused on AI-powered solutions, teamwork and problem-solving.
Brings together the Young AI Leaders and Data Science MSU communities to grow innovation, networking and skills in Gweru.

items.label: From Prompt to Production
items.detail: Sat, Apr 25 · American Corner, Gweru — building applications with no-code, using Google AI Studio.

more.meta: Sat, Apr 25, 9:00 AM · American Corner, Gweru
body: A hands-on workshop on moving from idea to execution — turning simple prompts into fully functional applications with Google AI Studio, no coding required.
Covers writing effective prompts, using AI to generate application logic, and building and testing a working app end to end.
Open to students, entrepreneurs, professionals and anyone curious about AI, innovation or digital product creation — just bring a laptop.

items.label: Prompting Like A Pro
items.detail: Sat, Feb 28 · American Corner, Gweru — the second session in the hub's AI Literacy series.

more.meta: Sat, Feb 28, 10:00 AM · American Corner, Gweru
body: The second session in the hub's AI Literacy series, focused squarely on prompt engineering for generative AI.
After the first session covered inputs, outputs and the GIGO principle, this one dug into crafting effective prompts and instructing AI systems on the task at hand.
Held at American Corner in Gweru, with 23 attendees.

items.label: Understanding AI Beyond the Hype
items.detail: Feb 11 · Online via Zoom — the opening session of a six-part AI Literacy series.

body: The inaugural session of a six-part AI Literacy series, developed with American Space Gweru to demystify AI for general audiences.
A practical, no-jargon look at data, algorithms and machine learning — how they show up in apps and platforms people already use.
Set the foundation for the rest of the series, no technical background required.

items.label: All events on Luma
items.detail: Every hub event, past and upcoming, is listed on our Luma calendar — follow it to catch the next one.

items.label: AI for Good · ITU
items.detail: The Young AI Leaders Community operates under ITU's AI for Good initiative — connecting African youth tech hubs to a United Nations platform for global impact.

body: The Young AI Leaders Community operates under ITU's AI for Good — a United Nations platform connecting AI innovators with the Sustainable Development Goals.
For Gweru, that means our members' local projects plug into a global network: shared learning, shared standards and a stage for Zimbabwean work.
It also keeps us honest — every project must answer the question: AI for good, for whom?

items.label: Local institutions
items.detail: Collaborating with universities, schools and innovation spaces across Zimbabwe and Southern Africa to grow African tech capacity from the ground up.

body: The hub collaborates with universities, schools and innovation spaces across Zimbabwe and Southern Africa to grow tech capacity from the ground up.
Partnerships range from guest workshops and student challenges to shared space and mentorship — whatever moves local AI literacy forward.
If your institution wants to work with the hub, reach out through the Contact anchor.

items.label: Become a partner
items.detail: Support youth-led, ethical AI and tech innovation in Africa — sponsor a project, host a workshop or mentor our members.

body: Support youth-led, ethical AI and tech innovation in Africa — sponsor a project, host a workshop, offer mentorship or open a venue.
Partners get a direct line into a community of up to 50 young AI leaders building real, SDG-aligned projects in the Midlands.
Start the conversation through the Contact anchor — we will find the shape of partnership that fits.
Reach out via email : yail.gweru@gmail.com
